education.degree: Bachelor of Science in Computer Science

tasks: Implemented code for lung cancer CT screening to enhance the efficiency of pulmonary nodule detection.
Modified and optimized deep learning algorithms in C++ and Python, and extracted patient data for analysis.
Spearheaded the establishment of the Girls Who Code College Loop at RPI, organizing events to support female participation in coding activities.
Responsible for overseeing meetings and organizing events to support female participation in tech.
Utilized YOLOv8 and NumPy for the endangered zebra population in Zambia monitoring via computer vision.
Designed user-friendly web apps for the Wild Me project, integrating machine learning for better data visualization and efficient conservation.
Designed a mobile application for the RPI President, providing direct access to schedules and bookings on campus.
Collaborated with cross-functional teams to build REST APIs in Express.js and MongoDB, enabling seamless event data integration with two other event-scheduling mobile applications.
Contributed to the front-end development of a mobile app for the annual RPI Hackathon, streamlining participant registration and providing clear access to hackathon statistics.
Implemented new features using Figma and React, and managed version control via GitHub repositories.

conferences: Grace Hopper Celebration for Women in Computing
Girls Who Code Hiring Winter Summit
clubs: Member of Girls Who Code Association at RPI
